title: paeonia mascula subsp. kesrouanensis
intro: Paeonia mascula subsp. kesrouanensis is a subspecies of the Paeonia mascula plant, commonly known as the oriental peony or Kesrouan peony, which is a type of flowering plant belonging to the peony family. Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with Paeonia mascula subsp. kesrouanensis include its potential anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, the roots of this plant are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
